rationale:
Reviewed against the frozen v15 Task 4a scope (v12 + AM-40 + AM-41) clause by clause; the implementation is faithful within frozen scope and I found no genuine scope defect. Key verifications:

(a) INVENTORY: PACKAGE_ROOTS is exactly the five spine packages and PACKAGE_ROOTS_EXCLUDE exactly the three products, both frozen and sorted; test-inventory §7 mechanically enumerates tracked package.json directories via a pinned no-shell `git ls-files` and proves discovered == union AND intersection == ∅, exact arrays asserted (AM-40 as written, including the tracked-not-filesystem discovery basis). DOC_ROOTS, docs/runs + self-weave exclusions, CONTRACT_FILES (D31, sorted, existence-proven), RUN_SOURCES, five weaver ids + integer versions all committed. REQUIRED_INVENTORY_IDS matches the frozen normative table verbatim with a deepEqual unit plus sorted/unique/frozen assertions. LOADER_CAPABLE_BUILTIN_SAFE_EXPORTS is deep-frozen with exact key set {module, node:module}, each value exactly ["builtinModules","isBuiltin"], with deep-equal, exact-key/value, deep-freeze, and mutation-fails-and-unchanged units (D32). LEDGER_SOURCES committed as the exact reviewed empty set with an explicit rationale and shape-enforcing unit — a committed closed source inventory, not a deferred placeholder, which is within the task text's requirement to commit "exact configured ledger files plus adapter ids."

(b) D17/AM-17: WEAVER_IMPL_FILES covers exactly git and code; every named file exists (test-inventory §4 and the closure harness's existence check); no orchestrator or test/doc/ledger inventory present.

(c) UTIL SUBSTRATE: one classifier (classifyModuleLoads) and one resolver (resolveRelativeSpecifier) in util.mjs; test-closure §9 proves single definition across the repo, that code.mjs imports the shared resolver by name, that deriveAcceptedClosure's own body calls both, and that no other module appends extensions (AM-34 test 19 satisfied for the consumers that exist at this PR; the advisory scanner is Task 5). The lexer is dependency-free, comment/string/template/regex-aware with previous-significant-token regex-vs-division including control-head parens, postfix ++/--, and `of` handling — each pinned by units. Phase-1 export grammar is exactly the four forms; unsupported categories (default, star, list, destructuring, let/var, multi-declarator) each warn with no symbol. Walker rejects root escape, symlinked roots/components/ancestors, and symlinked entries (fail-closed throw rather than skip — the stricter reading of "rejects rather than following"). Counted iterator counts only on completed consumption, exhaustion only on natural completion, cardinality = configured length, accounting reachable only through the driver-held accessor (units include a probe that the iterable leaks nothing). physicalContainment lstats every component including the original uncollapsed chain (AM-41(c)) and never follows symlinks to decide; unit fixtures cover symlinked allowed root, nested parent, escape-via-symlink, plain accept, and the link/../x collapse case. Git wrapper permits exactly the five frozen shapes with order/duplication/extra-arg rejection, canonical non-option path args, no shell, and a closed spawn-option set that drops caller env/cwd/argv0 (a hardening consistent with, not an expansion of, the frozen wrapper contract).

(d) AM-41 PROFILE: b1–b6 each detected and rejected with the stable `unsupported-module-lexical-profile` diagnostic — bare CR/U+2028/U+2029 (mode-independent scan), non-leading or second hashbang, HTML comment openers, string line-continuation and legacy/octal escapes (with \0-not-before-digit admitted), string-literal specifier/alias names, and unterminated strings/comments/templates/truncated accepted forms (unclosed calls and from-clauses without specifiers). One optional leading shebang at byte 0 admitted and stripped, LF/CRLF both tested; #! inside string/comment correctly in-profile. Supported-profile variation (whitespace, comments, options+trailing commas, keyword-named aliases, contextual `from`, member/private/property/meta lookalikes) is unit-covered. Both AM-41(d) properties are proven, and out-of-profile files fail the closure with no result (test-closure §14 covers entry and traversed member).

(e) CLOSURE TEST: derives each committed weaver closure with the shared classifier/resolver and proves equality (never trusts), via one harness shared between the real-inventory check and the failure fixtures; per-form omission fixtures cover every accepted form including export*as-ns, keyword-named alias, and whitespace module.require; recursion/cycles/unreachable-dynamic-import/permitted-merkle-dag-traversal covered; missing/extra/nonexistent-inventory, unresolved, ambiguous-extension, non-regular, escape, forbidden, symlink (junction-based, must-run), entry-level checks, non-literal and lookalike no-edge cases all present. §12 additionally asserts exact real-repo closures and that registry/inventory are leaves. The closure resolver traverses any explicit extension (extensions:"any") per the AM-41 note that .cjs is an example, with .json/.js fixtures — faithful to D33's "whatever the code reaches" while keeping extensionless ambiguous-fatal.

(f) GIT WEAVER: emits exactly `log -S<symbol> --format=%H --reverse -- <path>` and `log --format=%H --reverse -- <path>` (unit asserts exact argv), first result, source_ref git:<same-sha>, every line validated as full SHA (blank/internal-blank/bare-CR fatal; CRLF tolerated — a defensible reading of "every output line is validated as a full SHA" since CRLF is a line terminator, not line content, and it is explicitly pinned by units), empty output warns with no edge, producer-attributed warnings, byte-equal two-run determinism, both counted sources exhausted including the empty-source case, own-input validation before argv splice with a spy-runner proof, and closure-preserving imports (registry only).

(g) CODE WEAVER: consumes package-modules; explicit-.mjs extraction grammar retained in code.mjs via the shared resolver's extension parameter; all four depends-on endpoint shapes fixture-proven with alias resolution, no-export named-import preserved as repository-file -> code-symbol (not downgraded), namespace/side-effect/non-seeded-name file-level threading, dedup, unused-import no-edge, comment/string/regex non-uses, unrepresentable-consumer ONLY for unresolvable specifiers (missing .mjs or resolving outside seeded roots) with non-.mjs silent-precedence pinned, symlink/escape targets fail closed as physical-policy violations rather than warnings (fail-closed is the conservative direction and consistent with D21/D33 posture), and byte-equal two-run determinism.

CONSTRAINTS: zero runtime deps, ESM, node:-stdlib + clotho-relative imports only (verified by the gate's imports-permitted check), diff confined to clotho/, spine untouched. The deterministic gate passed all 15 checks.

Judgment items I weighed and accepted as within-scope readings rather than defects: (1) CRLF tolerance in SHA parsing — pinned, deterministic, and validates every line's content as a full SHA; (2) LEDGER_SOURCES = [] — the task requires committing the exact configured set, and the exact reviewed set is empty, with tests enforcing shape if ever nonempty; (3) closure traversal of any explicit extension — mandated by the AM-41 scope note itself; (4) DOC_WEAVER_EXCLUDE/GLOBAL_EXCLUDE committed but consumed later — the task requires committing them now while doc-weaver/driver land in 4b/5; (5) header comments citing "plan v13 (v12 + AM-40)" while the authoritative frozen scope is v15 (adding AM-41) — a cosmetic label in comments only; the AM-41 behavior itself is fully implemented and tested, and the manifest's proving references are content addresses, not these labels. Confidence is medium rather than high solely because the review is over the presented artifact text (I could not execute the suite myself), and the lexer's soundness argument rests on the extensive but necessarily finite unit pinning; within the enforced closed profile that risk is acceptable.
